title: Q moon
wikitext:
[]: Q moon             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Mar 22, 2016 12:14 pm As we can all remember, the moon in Q appears to have gone under some rather.........drastic changes SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 22, 2016 12:24 pm I believe that second image is from the teaser when Q was announced, and isn't actually in the final film.

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Tue Mar 22, 2016 12:50 pm It's not? Aw man, this could've made for some neat speculation

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 22, 2016 1:28 pm The unused footage does bring a question to mind, though... Is the giant blood splash something that had always been there, or something that happened as a result of 3I? We see at the end of 1.0 (and 2.0, too?) that the stain is too small to be visible from Earth. Do the 3.0 preview and final actually show the same part of the moon's surface, though? Can someone whose Eva archives are in better shape than mine check?

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by unitM » Tue Mar 22, 2016 1:31 pm Can someone post the footage?

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Mar 22, 2016 2:20 pm Reichu wrote: The unused footage does bring a question to mind, though... Is the giant blood splash something that had always been there, or something that happened as a result of 3I? We see at the end of 1.0 (and 2.0, too?) that the stain is too small to be visible from Earth.

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 22, 2016 3:27 pm https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3nPKs3rQ9s4

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Mar 22, 2016 8:03 pm It doesn't match the blood splats on the moon in 3.0. It's probably a mock-up of the streak that was already there, or perhaps a very early version of the blood-coated moon that got expanded in the making. Unit 02's prosthetic arm is just a normal Eva arm colored grey.

[]: Re: Q moon             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Wed Mar 23, 2016 10:01 pm Q Moon... You saw me standin' alone... Without a dream in my heart... Without a love of my own... (It had to be done. ) But yeah, the second picture is probably not in 3.33. I don't remember them ever getting that close to the moon. In the second pic the moon is much further from the earth (but still not where it should be). In the first one it's close enough to leech some of the atmosphere away from the earth via gravity. By the way, the moon is actually a lot further away from the earth than it appears to be from our point of view: http://planetfacts.org/wp-content/uploa ... -Earth.jpg
